159	### Decision Q1: 2-chart cover of `ProjectiveLineBar`
160	
161	- **Mathlib idiom**: `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` specialised to `ι := Fin 2`,
162	  `f := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]`, `m := ![1, 1]`. **EXACT** signature
163	  the prover writes:
164	
165	  ```lean
166	  /-- The 2-chart cover of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`'s underlying scheme by the two
167	  affine charts `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. -/
168	  noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
169	      (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
170	    Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
171	      (ι := Fin 2)
172	      (f := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
173	      (m := ![1, 1])
174	      (f_deg := fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;>
175	        exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar _)
176	      (hm := fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> exact one_pos)
177	      (hf := by
178	        -- The irrelevant ideal = (X 0, X 1), span of the two generators.
179	        intro p hp
180	        rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff] at hp
181	        -- `p` is in degree-positive part; decompose into X-monomials and conclude.
182	        sorry  -- standard MvPolynomial decomposition; ~10 LOC
183	        )
184	  ```
185	
186	  The `hf` step needs to show that the irrelevant ideal of the standard ℕ-grading on
187	  `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` is contained in `Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — a standard
188	  decomposition argument via `MvPolynomial.support_X`. **This is the only
189	  not-completely-trivial sub-step**; the rest is `fin_cases`.
190	
191	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`**.
192	- **Concrete checklist for the prover**: name the cover
193	  `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (NOT `projectiveLineBar...Cover`); use `Fin 2`
194	  as index (not `PNat × _` like the all-generators `Proj.affineOpenCover`).

197	### Decision Q2: `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`
198	
199	- **Mathlib idiom**: **NOT SHIPPED**. The previous iter-167 sketch said
200	  "~30 LOC, project owes". The deeper investigation at iter-168 reveals:
201	  `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is *not* a localization-of-the-graded-ring (it's
202	  the *degree-0 sub-ring* of `Localization (Submonoid.powers f)` selected by the
203	  homogeneous-fraction structure). So the iso must be built from the `Away.mk`
204	  +  `val_injective` API directly.
205	
206	  **The canonical Mathlib path is the universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`**:
207	
208	  ```lean
209	  /-- The chart-`i` ring `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≅ k̄[u]` where `u = X (1-i) / X i`. -/
210	  noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso
211	      (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
212	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
213	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
214	        MvPolynomial Unit kbar where
215	    -- Inverse direction (THE EASY HALF — universal property of MvPolynomial Unit):
216	    invFun := MvPolynomial.aeval (R := kbar)
217	      (S₁ := HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X i))
218	      (fun _ => HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
219	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
220	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) 1
221	        (MvPolynomial.X (jOf i))                  -- jOf i := if i = 0 then 1 else 0
222	        (by simp; exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (jOf i)))
223	    -- Forward direction (THE HARD HALF — defined by `Quotient.lift` via `val_injective`):
224	    toFun := Quotient.lift
225	      (fun ⟨n, ⟨num, hnum⟩, ⟨den, hden⟩, ⟨k, hk⟩⟩ =>
226	        -- evaluate the fraction `num / X i ^ k` at X i ↦ 1, X (jOf i) ↦ X ()
227	        MvPolynomial.eval (fun j => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
228	                                     else MvPolynomial.X ()) num)
229	      (by
230	        intro x y h
231	        -- The equivalence relation is `embedding x = embedding y`, both views in
232	        -- `Localization (powers (X i))`. Use the well-defined ring map
233	        -- `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ → MvPolynomial Unit k̄`, `X i ↦ 1`, `X (jOf i) ↦ X ()`
234	        -- and the fact that `X i ↦ 1` makes powers of `X i` into 1 (units), so the
235	        -- map factors through Localization.
236	        sorry  -- ~10-15 LOC: invoke MvPolynomial.eval's well-definedness
237	        )
238	    map_one' := by simp [HomogeneousLocalization.one_eq, Quotient.lift]
239	    map_mul' := by
240	      rintro ⟨_⟩ ⟨_⟩
241	      simp [Quotient.lift]; ring  -- standard
242	    map_add' := by
243	      rintro ⟨_⟩ ⟨_⟩
244	      simp [Quotient.lift]; ring  -- standard
245	    map_zero' := by simp [HomogeneousLocalization.zero_eq, Quotient.lift]
246	    left_inv := by
247	      rintro ⟨n, ⟨num, hnum⟩, ⟨den, hden⟩, ⟨k, rfl⟩⟩
248	      -- Round-trip: forward sends to `eval`; backward sends back to `Away.mk`.
249	      -- Use `HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective` to compare in `Localization`.
250	      apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
251	      sorry  -- ~15 LOC: chase via `Away.val_mk` + `Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff`
252	    right_inv := by
253	      intro p
254	      -- Round-trip: backward sends to `Away.mk` of `aeval`; forward evaluates.
255	      induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
256	      | C r => simp [MvPolynomial.aeval_C]; rfl
257	      | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
258	      | mul_X p _ hp => simp [hp]; ring
259	  ```
260	
261	  where `jOf : Fin 2 → Fin 2` is the "swap" `if i = 0 then 1 else 0`.
262	
263	- **Mathlib gap depth**: this is NOT shipped at all. The iter-167 file's "~30 LOC"
264	  estimate was too optimistic — the realistic count is **60-90 LOC** because:
265	  - The "forward" map's well-definedness goes through `Quotient.lift`'s
266	    well-definedness hypothesis on the embedding into `Localization`, which is
267	    where the actual ring-theoretic content lives.
268	  - `MvPolynomial.eval` is over a polynomial-ring substitution that the `Quotient.lift`
269	    needs to commute with — this is **not a single Mathlib lemma**.
270	  - The `left_inv` round-trip needs `HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective` plus
271	    a direct comparison in `Localization`.
272	
273	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**. The project owes this helper. It is
274	  upstream-able as a single ~80-LOC file `Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization.PolynomialQuotient`
275	  (or similar). Out of scope for landing in Mathlib at iter-168; landing in the
276	  project is required for `gmScalingP1`.
277	
278	  **PROCEED with the recipe above**, but treat the LOC estimate as ~60-90, not
279	  ~30. The previous file's optimistic estimate is what blocked iter-167 — the
280	  prover ran out of budget on a "30 LOC" item that was 3x larger.
281	
282	- **Alternative considered and rejected**: use `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top`
283	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:1064`) +
284	  `Algebra.adjoin_eq_top_iff_isIso_aeval`. **Rejected**: this still requires

288	### Decision Q3: chart-side morphism via `pullbackSpecIso`
289	
290	- **Setup**:
291	  - Chart `i` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left` = `Spec (Away 𝒜 (X i))`. After Q2's
292	    iso, identify with `Spec (MvPolynomial Unit k̄)`.
293	  - `Gm.left = Spec GmRing = Spec (Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄))`.
294	  - Product `chart_i ⊗ Gm` in `Over (Spec k̄)` = pullback of the two structure
295	    maps to `Spec k̄`. Via `pullbackSpecIso`, this is
296	    `Spec ((MvPolynomial Unit k̄) ⊗_{k̄} GmRing)`.
297	
298	- **The chart-1 morphism (the one needed by collapse-at-zero)**:
299	  Ring map at chart 1:
300	  ```
301	  φ₁ : MvPolynomial Unit k̄  →+*  MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗_{k̄} GmRing
302	  φ₁(X ()) = X () ⊗ₜ algebraMap kbar GmRing 1 * 1 ⊗ₜ X ()_Gm   -- = u·λ
303	  ```
304	  built concretely via `MvPolynomial.aeval`:
305	  ```lean
306	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
307	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
308	    (MvPolynomial.aeval
309	      (R := kbar) (σ := Unit)
310	      (S₁ := MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)
311	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
312	                  (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar) 1) *
313	                (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
314	                  (algebraMap _ (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))).toRingHom
315	  ```
316	
317	  **WARNING**: this is hard to read but the simple way to spell it is "u ↦ u·λ
318	  in the tensor product", which by `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul` equals
319	  `(u ⊗ 1) * (1 ⊗ λ) = u ⊗ λ`. So a cleaner spelling:
320	
321	  ```lean
322	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap :
323	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
324	    MvPolynomial.eval₂RingHom
325	      (algebraMap kbar _)
326	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
327	                  (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
328	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))
329	  ```
330	
331	  (Uses `MvPolynomial.eval₂RingHom` to send `X () ↦ u ⊗ λ` and `kbar ↦ kbar ⊗ 1`.)
332	
333	- **The chart-side morphism in `Scheme`**:
334	  ```lean
335	  -- chart 1's map "(u, λ) ↦ λ·u" — into ProjectiveLineBarScheme directly:
336	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
337	      pullback
338	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))))
339	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))) ⟶
340	        ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
341	    -- 1. Bridge: pullback ≅ Spec (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)
342	    (pullbackSpecIso kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).hom ≫
343	    -- 2. The ring map: u ↦ u ⊗ λ, defined in `gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap`
344	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap kbar)) ≫
345	    -- 3. The reverse iso k[u] ≃+* Away 𝒜 (X 1)
346	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).symm) ≫
347	    -- 4. The awayι into Proj
348	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)
349	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) one_pos
350	  ```
351	
352	  **WARNING (subtle)**: the "chart of `ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`" is NOT literally
353	  `pullback (Spec.map _) (Spec.map _)`; it's the `Over (Spec k̄)`-level product
354	  `chart_i ⊗ Gm`, whose underlying `Scheme.pullback` is `pullback awayι.hom Gm.hom`
355	  (NOT `pullback (algebraMap _ _) (algebraMap _ _)`). To bridge, the prover must
356	  use the explicit description of `(chart_i ⊗ Gm).left`:
357	
358	  ```lean
359	  (Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i))) ⊗ Gm kbar).left =
360	    pullback (chart structure map) (Gm structure map)
361	  ```
362	
363	  Both structure maps factor through `Spec k̄`, so by uniqueness of pullback this
364	  IS isomorphic to `pullback (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i))))
365	  (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar GmRing))`, which is what `pullbackSpecIso` expects.
366	  Practically, the prover writes the chart map at the `Over (Spec k̄)` level via
367	  `Over.homMk` and lets the `Over`-pullback API do the bridging.
368	
369	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `pullbackSpecIso` + ring map + reverse Q2-iso + `awayι`**.
370	  The ring map's definition is short (~5 LOC); the wrapping through `Over` /
371	  `pullbackSpecIso` is `~15 LOC`. **Critical**: the prover MUST work in
372	  `Over (Spec k̄)` from the start, or the `(chart ⊗ Gm)`-vs-`pullback awayι Gm.hom`
373	  mismatch will require manual bridging at every `simp`.

375	- **The chart-0 morphism (analogous, with `λ⁻¹` instead of `λ`)**:

379	  -- `Localization.Away`-invertible-element witness; `Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Away/Basic.lean`).

382	      (algebraMap kbar _)
383	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ

390	### Decision Q4: cross-chart agreement equation
391	
392	- **What `glueMorphisms` requires** (per `Gluing.lean:436`):
393	  ```
394	  ∀ x y : 𝒰.I₀, pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y) ≫ f x = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ f y
395	  ```
396	  For our 2-element cover `𝒰 = projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.pullback₁ pullback.fst`,
397	  the `x = y` cases are trivial (`pullback.fst = pullback.snd` on the diagonal,
398	  up to `pullback.condition`). The `(0, 1)` and `(1, 0)` cases are the real
399	  agreement check.
400	
401	- **The (0, 1)-case**: on the intersection `D₊(X 0) ∩ D₊(X 1) = D₊(X 0 · X 1)`,
402	  affine ring `Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)`. Via Mathlib's `Proj.Away.isLocalization_mul`
403	  (`RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:883`), this is
404	  `Localization.Away (isLocalizationElem (X 0)_deg (X 1)_deg)` on top of
405	  `Away 𝒜 (X 0)`, which after Q2's chart-0 iso is
406	  `Localization.Away (X ())` on top of `MvPolynomial Unit k̄`, i.e.
407	  `Localization.Away t = k̄[t, t⁻¹]`.
408	
409	  After tensoring with `GmRing = k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]`, the intersection ring is
410	  `k̄[t, t⁻¹] ⊗_{k̄} k̄[λ, λ⁻¹] = k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹]`.
411	
412	  **The ring-level equation**: both chart maps, restricted to the intersection,
413	  send `u = X 0 / X 1 ∈ Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)` (where `u = 1/t` on chart-0 side,
414	  `u = u` on chart-1 side) to the same expression `t ↦ λ·u ↔ λ/t` on chart-0
415	  and to `u ↦ λ·u` on chart-1. They're equal because `λ·u = λ/t = λ·(1/t)`.
416	
417	  **Practical form** (what the prover types):
418	
419	  ```lean
420	  -- For x = 0, y = 1 (the cross case):
421	  · -- Goal: pullback.fst _ _ ≫ chart0_map = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ chart1_map
422	    -- on Spec(MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ GmRing) (~)
423	    -- The two ring maps coincide on the (1-1)/(1-0) coordinate after multiplying by t
424	    -- (= passing through Localization.Away t). Use:
425	    apply (pullbackSpecIso _ _ _).hom.eq_of_comp_left  -- or similar
426	    -- then unfold and apply `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` + `t * (1/t) = 1`.
427	    sorry
428	  ```
429	
430	  **The actual ring-level identity**: in `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗
431	  MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ GmRing`, when restricted to the chart-intersection
432	  localization, the chart-0 expression `(1/t) ⊗ λ⁻¹` and the chart-1 expression
433	  `u ⊗ λ` get identified via the gluing iso `t = 1/u, u = 1/t`. Concretely, on
434	  the intersection ring `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing`, the chart-0 map sends
435	  `1/t ↦ (1/t)·λ⁻¹` and the chart-1 map (after `t = 1/u` substitution) sends
436	  `u ↦ u·λ = (1/t)·λ`. **They differ by a factor of `λ²`**. Wait — that's wrong.
437	  Let me recompute.
438	
439	  Actually: chart-0 affine coord t = X 1/X 0, chart-1 affine coord u = X 0/X 1.
440	  So on the intersection, `t·u = 1`, i.e. `u = 1/t`. Under σ_×:
441	  - Chart-0: `t ↦ t/λ` (NOT `t ↦ λ·t`). After convention chosen above
442	    (`σ_× = [λX 0 : X 1]`): `t = X 1/X 0 ↦ X 1/(λ X 0) = t/λ`.
443	  - Chart-1: `u ↦ λ·u`.
444	  - On intersection: `λ·u = λ·(1/t) = λ/t`, vs `t ↦ t/λ` means `u = 1/t ↦ 1/(t/λ) = λ/t`. **EQUAL.** ✓
445	
446	  So the cross-chart agreement reduces to `λ·u = 1/(t/λ)`, which after
447	  `u = 1/t` and `Localization.Away t`'s invertibility is `λ·(1/t)·t = λ`, true.
448	
449	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with the explicit ring-level identity** `λ·u = (1/t)·λ`
450	  inside `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing`. The proof script is:
451	  ```lean
452	  · -- Cross-chart agreement at (i, j) = (0, 1)
453	    -- Goal: pullback.fst _ _ ≫ chart0_map = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ chart1_map
454	    -- Reduce both sides to a Spec.map of the SAME ring map on
455	    -- Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗_{k̄} GmRing).
456	    -- This is a `pullbackSpecIso` chain + a `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` /
457	    -- IsLocalization.lift_uniq closure.
458	    ext1
459	    · -- on Spec coord (1/t)
460	      simp [gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap, gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap,
461	        pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst, pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd,
462	        Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, mul_comm]
463	      -- residual: `t · u = 1` in `Localization.Away t`. Use
464	      -- `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf` or `Localization.Away.invSelf_mul`.
465	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC of algebra
466	  ```
467	  Estimated body: ~40 LOC total for both cross-chart cases (one (0,1) case +
468	  one (1,0) case, the latter by symmetry).

470	### Decision Q5: `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` signature + bridging
471	
472	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` (`Gluing.lean:436`) takes
473	  `𝒰 : OpenCover.{v} X` (a `Cover (precoverage @IsOpenImmersion)`). Bridge from
474	  `AffineOpenCover` to `Cover`:
475	  ```lean
476	  -- In our setting we want to glue on (ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm).left.
477	  -- We have Lane A's projectiveLineBarAffineCover : (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover
478	  -- (indexed by Fin 2). Pull this back along (ProjectiveLineBar.hom) ⊗ id (or
479	  -- equivalently along pullback.fst inside (ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm).left).
480	  --
481	  -- The bridge: AffineOpenCover.openCover → OpenCover.
482	  -- Then OpenCover.pullback₁ produces the cover of the product.
483	  noncomputable def projGmCover : ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
484	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
485	      (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
486	  ```
487	  **Chosen cover index**: `Fin 2` (inherited from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`).
488	  Each chart `i : Fin 2` becomes
489	  `pullback (pullback.fst (PLB).hom (Gm).hom) (awayι ... (X i) ...)`.
490	
491	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. The bridge is one line; the cover indexing is `Fin 2`
492	  (NOT a product type — the `pullback₁` keeps the original cover's index).

494	### Decision Q6: `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body
495	
496	- **Setup**: once `gmScalingP1` is concrete (Q3-Q5), the collapse-at-zero lemma
497	  is a chart-level computation. `zeroPt : 𝟙_ ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` is
498	  realized via `pointOfVec (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 _`, so:
499	  - `zeroPt`'s underlying scheme map: `Spec k̄ ⟶ Proj 𝒜` via
500	    `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the eval `X 0 ↦ 0, X 1 ↦ 1`.
501	  - **Critically**: this factors through `awayι 𝒜 (X 1)` (chart-1 inclusion)
502	    because `v 1 = 1` is the unit coordinate. The factorisation is a `Spec.map`
503	    of the eval map `Away 𝒜 (X 1) → k̄`, which under Q2's iso is
504	    `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ → k̄`, sending `u ↦ 0` (since `zeroPt` has affine
505	    coord `u = X 0 / X 1 = 0 / 1 = 0`).
506	
507	- **The collapse-at-zero statement** (verbatim from L472):
508	  ```lean
509	  lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
510	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
511	          gmScalingP1 kbar =
512	        toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar
513	  ```
514	
515	- **Proof strategy**: use `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` (`Gluing.lean:448`) on the
516	  `(Gm).left.OpenCover` consisting of the single chart `𝟙 (Gm).left`. Since `Gm`
517	  is affine (`gm_isAffine`), this reduces to checking the equation at the
518	  ring level after applying `Spec.map`.
519	
520	  **Actually simpler**: factor `lift (toUnit ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm` through
521	  the chart-1 chart of the cover (since `zeroPt` lives in chart-1), then use
522	  `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` to compute `chart_1.f ≫ gmScalingP1 = chart_1.morphism`.
523	
524	  ```lean
525	  lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
526	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
527	          gmScalingP1 kbar =
528	        toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
529	    -- Step 1: `lift (toUnit ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)` factors through chart-1 of
530	    -- (ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm).
531	    -- Step 2: `gmScalingP1`'s chart-1 component is the ring map `u ↦ u·λ`.
532	    -- Step 3: precomposing with `zeroPt → chart-1` (which sends `u ↦ 0`) gives
533	    -- the ring map `u ↦ 0·λ = 0`, i.e. the constant `0`.
534	    -- Step 4: that's exactly `toUnit ≫ zeroPt`.
535	    --
536	    -- Concretely, by `Scheme.Hom.ext` (or `Over.hom_ext`) reduce to
537	    -- `Spec`-level, then use the chart-1 `Spec.map` chain.
538	    apply Over.hom_ext
539	    -- Or: factor through chart-1 and apply `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`.
540	    -- The detailed unfold:
541	    --   chart-1 ring map: φ₁(u) = u ⊗ λ ∈ k̄[u] ⊗ GmRing
542	    --   precompose with `Spec(k̄[u]/⟨u⟩) ⟶ Spec(k̄[u])` (the u ↦ 0 eval).
543	    --   In the ring: u ⊗ λ ↦ 0 ⊗ λ = 0 (since 0 ⊗ anything = 0 in tensor product).
544	    -- That's the ring map `u ↦ 0`, i.e. the constant map at `u = 0`.
545	    sorry  -- ~30-40 LOC of chart-1 chase
546	  ```
547	
548	  The "actual `rw` / `simp` set" the prover should drive the residual to:
549	  - `MvPolynomial.aeval_X` / `MvPolynomial.eval₂_X` (to evaluate the ring map
550	    on the generator).
551	  - `Algebra.TensorProduct.zero_tmul` / `TensorProduct.zero_tmul` (`0 ⊗ x = 0`).
552	  - `pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst` / `_snd` (to commute `Spec.map` with `pullback.fst/snd`).
553	  - `Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen` + `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`
554	    (to identify the factorisation of `zeroPt` through chart-1).
555	
556	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED**. The proof is ~30-50 LOC, the long pole being the
557	  initial unfold of `gmScalingP1`'s concrete chart-1 form (Q3) and the
558	  factorisation of `zeroPt` through `awayι (X 1)` (which needs an explicit
559	  helper lemma `ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt_factors_through_chart1`).
560	
561	  **Concrete helper to land first** (~15 LOC):
562	  ```lean
563	  lemma ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt_left_factors_through_chart1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
564	      ∃ s : Spec (.of kbar) ⟶ Spec (.of (Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1))),
565	        (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar).left =
566	          s ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)
567	              (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) one_pos := by
568	    -- s is `Spec.map (eval X 1 ↦ 1, X 0 ↦ 0 on Away 𝒜 (X 1))`,
569	    -- which via Q2's iso is `Spec.map (X () ↦ 0 : k̄[u] →+* k̄)`.
570	    sorry
571	  ```
572	
573	  Use `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` (`Basic.lean:493`) to extract
574	  the chart-1 factorisation explicitly.
575

5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
11	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
12	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
13	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
14	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
15	% keystone directly). The headline thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
16	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
17	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
18	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
19	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
20	
21	The genus-$0$ arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
22	($J = \Spec k$); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
23	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$
24	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
25	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field $k$, so the
26	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
27	O(-2)) = 0$, hence $df = 0$, hence $f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
28	characteristic-$p$ Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant $f$ can have $df = 0$. The
29	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
30	
31	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
32	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
33	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
34	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
35	derives the genus-$0$ base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
36	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
37	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
38	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that $\mathbb P^1$ is
39	\emph{already complete} and $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
40	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
41	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut} (the project's
42	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
43	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
44	\mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (the M\"obius scaling fixing both $0$ and
45	$\infty$), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
46	G_m$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising $f(0) = \eta_A$ and composing $h := \sigma_\times
47	\fatsemi f$, one applies additivity of $\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
48	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
49	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ proper, $W = \mathbb G_m$, and
50	base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$. Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of
51	scaling, the $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)$ is
52	\emph{constant} at $\eta_A$, so the decomposition collapses to $h = p \fatsemi f$, i.e.\
53	$f(\lambda x) = f(x)$. Specialising $x = 1$ gives that $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant, and since
54	$\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ with $A$ separated, $f$ is constant
55	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
56	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
57	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
58	$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$, and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
59	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical $\mathbb G_a$-additive
60	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility
61	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
62	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$ critical path. The corollary that a
63	pointed regular map of abelian
64	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
65	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-$0$ path; Milne's
66	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
67	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-$0$ critical path
68	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
69	gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant
70	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-$0$
71	curves (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}, $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$) yields the
72	headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
73	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
74	neither Serre duality nor the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is
75	fully decoupled from Route~A.
76	
77	\medskip
78	
79	Throughout, $\bar k$ is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
80	\emph{abelian variety} over $\bar k$ is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
81	project's Lean encoding $A$ is an object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ carrying
82	$\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, and $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$.
83	
84	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
85	
86	\begin{lemma}

    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra

/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.

  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))

/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.**

194	  /-- index set of an affine cover of a scheme `S` -/

    (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:493`). The latter is cleanest but needs the
    intersection identified as `D₊(X 0 · X 1)` in the chart cover, requiring
    `Proj.basicOpen`-cover-restriction lemmas not yet specialised to
    `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`.
  * Total LOC estimate: ~150-200 for the chart maps alone, plus ~50 for the cocycle.

(b) **Functoriality-of-Proj route (NEW, not in iter-169 plan).** Mathlib ships
`Proj.map : (𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ) → (Proj ℬ ⟶ Proj 𝒜)` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Functor.lean:144`).
Take `𝒜 = MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar` and `ℬ = MvPolynomial (Fin 2) (GmRing kbar)` (both
with the standard ℕ-grading); the graded ring map `φ : 𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ` with `X 0 ↦ t · X 0`,
`X 1 ↦ X 1` is well-defined. This gives `Proj.map φ : Proj ℬ → Proj 𝒜 = ℙ¹`. The
remaining step — identifying `Proj ℬ ≅ ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm` — requires the
**relative-Proj base-change iso**
`Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) R) ≅ Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) k) ×_{Spec k} Spec R`
which is NOT in Mathlib (verified iter-169: no `Proj.iso_pullback_Spec`,
`ProjPolynomial`, or `relativeProj` declarations exist).

**Specific blocking technical reason (per objectives criterion):** route (a) is
~200 LOC of TensorProduct + HomogeneousLocalization.Away ring-instance plumbing that has
no in-tree precedent; route (b) is gated on a missing relative-Proj iso in Mathlib that
would be a multi-iter upstream PR. Neither closes within an iter-169 budget consistent
with not breaking the build.

**Iter-170 escalation surface (per the iter-169 plan's commitment):** PRIMARY did not
land; the user-escalation trigger is now armed via the 5th-iter deferral commitment.
Options surfaced for the planner:
1. Commit to upstream Mathlib sub-build PR `HomogeneousLocalization.AwayTensor` providing
   the `CommRing/Algebra` instance and the `Proj.relative` iso (~5-iter detour).
2. Accept `[CharZero]` and use `rigidity_over_kbar` as the genus-0 char-0-only artifact
   (the fallback (a) `[CharZero]`-gated route).
3. Build the chart-glue inline (~200-300 LOC dedicated to this single body across 2-3
   iters); current iter-169 prover budget cannot accommodate.

Scaffold body retained as typed `sorry` per protocol "never weaken the type to dodge the
proof". -/
def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  sorry

/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.

This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that
`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.

**Status (iter-169 PARTIAL/escalation):** body remains `sorry`, gated on `gmScalingP1`'s
own body landing. Once `gmScalingP1` ships a concrete construction (via either route
(a) chart-glue or route (b) the relative-Proj iso, see `gmScalingP1`'s docstring), this
collapse lemma reduces to a chart-level ring-map computation: in chart 1 (where
`zeroPt = [0 : 1]` factors), the chart-1 ring map sends `X 0 ↦ 0 · 1 ⊗ λ = 0` and
`X 1 ↦ 1 ⊗ 1 = 1`, so the composite at the `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` level evaluates
to `zeroPt` independent of `λ` — i.e. constant in `Gm`. The helper
`zeroPt_factors_through_chart1` (~15 LOC, derived via
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`) is the cleanest discharge.

Iter-170 escalation note: this lemma's body discharge is **strictly downstream** of
`gmScalingP1`'s body — it cannot land before that one. -/
lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
        gmScalingP1 kbar =
      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
  sorry
input.new_string:
/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`

`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).

Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.

The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/

/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))

/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
        GmRing kbar))

/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)

/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.

**Status:** body remains a typed `sorry`. The construction requires the chart-ring iso
`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`, whose `aux_left` round-trip is still `sorry` in this
file (deferred infrastructure not on the genus-0 critical path). Internal to
`gmScalingP1` skeleton — each chart morphism is a top-level named declaration. -/
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
  sorry

/-- **Cocycle agreement for `gmScalingP1_chart`** on intersections of `(gmScalingP1_cover).f`.
The substantive `(0, 1)` / `(1, 0)` cross cases reduce on `D₊(X 0 · X 1)` to the ring-level
identity `λ·u = (1/t)·λ` in `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing` (where `t·u = 1`); the diagonal
`(0, 0)` / `(1, 1)` cases are trivial via `pullback.condition`.

**Status:** typed `sorry`, gated on `gmScalingP1_chart` having a concrete construction. -/
lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ∀ x y : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
          gmScalingP1_chart kbar x =
        pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫
          gmScalingP1_chart kbar y :=
  sorry

/-- **The over-structure coherence for the glued scheme map.** Asserts that the glued
morphism `(gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart … : (ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`
intertwines the structure maps to `Spec k̄`. Reduces to checking on each chart of the cover
(via `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext`) — on chart-`i`, both compositions land in `Spec k̄`, where
agreement is automatic from the way `gmScalingP1_chart i` is built (factoring through
`Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing))`).

**Status:** typed `sorry`, gated on `gmScalingP1_chart` having a concrete construction. -/
lemma gmScalingP1_over_coherence (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
        (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
        (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar) ≫
      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom :=
  sorry

/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.

The morphism is the bare scheme map `(x, λ) ↦ λ·x` (Möbius scaling fixing `0` and `∞`).
Built via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the 2-chart cover `gmScalingP1_cover` (the
pullback of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` along `pullback.fst`). The chart-`i` scheme
morphism `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i`, the cocycle agreement
`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar`, and the over-side coherence
`gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar` are top-level named declarations — body skeleton with
three internal `sorry`s, each at a named declaration (no buried sorries).

Consumed by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut: Cor 1.5 +
density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` + `ext_of_eqOnOpen`). The load-bearing fixed-point property
`σ_×(0, λ) = 0` is exposed by the companion `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`. -/
noncomputable def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  Over.homMk
    ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
      (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
      (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar))
    (gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar)

/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.

This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that
`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.

**Status:** typed `sorry`. Reduces (once `gmScalingP1_chart` is concrete) to the chart-1
ring-map computation: chart-1's ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` and `zeroPt` factors through
chart-1 at `u = 0`, so the composite at the `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` level evaluates to
`zeroPt` independently of `λ`. -/
lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
        gmScalingP1 kbar =
      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
  sorry

/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**

Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.

**Status:** typed `sorry` — substantive infrastructure for upstreaming the chart-ring iso
as `HomogeneousLocalization.PolynomialQuotient`. Required for closing `aux_left` via the
"cancel surjective" route. -/
private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) :=
  sorry

/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.

Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
      RingHom.id _ := by
  ext x
  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]

# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean — iter-171 Lane A

## Outcome — PARTIAL (acceptable per progress-critic `route171`)

**Body-first attack on `gmScalingP1` LANDED.** Three internal sorries hoisted to top-level
named declarations (`gmScalingP1_chart`, `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`,
`gmScalingP1_over_coherence`); body itself is now a concrete `Over.homMk` over
`Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`. PRIMARY deliverables all landed; SECONDARY refactored
`aux_left` into a real proof depending on one focused surjectivity helper.

- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` → exit 0 (sorry warnings only).
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` → exit 0 (downstream green).
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian` → exit 0.

### Sorry count on file

8 → 10 (net +2, body skeleton broken into 3 named internal sorries):

| Line | Declaration | Status |
|------|-------------|--------|
| 186  | `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` | EXISTING (Mathlib gap, off-path) |
| 193  | `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim` | EXISTING (Mathlib gap, off-path) |
| 372  | `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` | **NEW** — replaces L371 bare-sorry `aux_left`. Cancel-surjective helper. |
| 624  | `gm_grpObj` | EXISTING (iter-173 target via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`) |
| 695  | `gmScalingP1_chart` | **NEW** — chart-`i` scheme morphism (internal sorry #1) |
| 705  | `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` | **NEW** — cocycle agreement (internal sorry #2) |
| 721  | `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` | **NEW** — over-side structure-map coherence (internal sorry #3) |
| 762  | `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` | EXISTING (gated on chart concretization) |
| 842  | `gm_geomIrred` | EXISTING (Mathlib gap) |
| 872  | `projGm_isReduced` | EXISTING (gated on `aux_left` chain) |

`gmScalingP1` itself is no longer a `sorry` — it's a concrete `Over.homMk` invocation.
`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` is no longer a bare `sorry` — it now has a real
proof body via `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective`.

## What landed PRIMARY

### `algebraKbarAway` instance (line 87) — axiom-clean

```lean
noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
    Algebra kbar
      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
```

**Axioms:** `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified via `lean_verify`).

Unblocks `Algebra kbar (TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 _) (GmRing kbar))` synthesis via
`Algebra.TensorProduct.instCommRing` per `analogies/tensoraway-instance.md` Q2.

### Step A chart ring maps (lines 659, 668) — axiom-clean

```lean
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))

noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
        GmRing kbar))
```

**Axioms:** `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified).

Chart-1 sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ`, chart-0 sends `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (via `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf`).
Not yet consumed by the body skeleton (the chart scheme morphisms `gmScalingP1_chart` are
still sorry; once they're filled, they'll plug these into `pullbackSpecIso`).

### `gmScalingP1` body skeleton via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` (line 742)

```lean
noncomputable def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  Over.homMk
    ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).glueMorphisms
      (gmScalingP1_chart kbar)
      (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement kbar))
    (gmScalingP1_over_coherence kbar)
```

Body is concrete — `Over.homMk` of a `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` invocation. The three
internal sorries (`gmScalingP1_chart`, `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`,
`gmScalingP1_over_coherence`) are each top-level named declarations satisfying the
no-buried-sorry rule.

Supporting helper (axiom-clean):

```lean
noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
```

**Axioms of `gmScalingP1_cover`:** `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified).
**Axioms of `gmScalingP1` itself:** `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` —
sorry propagates through the three internal scaffold sorries, as expected for PARTIAL.

## What landed SECONDARY

### `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` rewritten (line 380)

Replaced bare `sorry` with cancel-surjective body:

```lean
private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
      RingHom.id _ := by
  ext x
  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
```

The cancel-surjective logic is now wired correctly; the **only** residual sorry on this
chain is `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (line 372), a focused
surjectivity claim with a clear closure path via `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top`
(`Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:1064`) specialised to
`d = 1`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`. The full close is mathematically
straightforward but ~60-80 LOC (decompose any `Away.mk hf n a ha` along the
`X 0^(d 0) · X 1^(d 1)` monomial basis of `𝒜 n`, observe each `Away.mk hf n (X 0^k X 1^(n-k))`
equals `α^k` on chart-1 / `α^(n-k)` on chart-0 where `α = isLocalizationElem`).

### Attempts table

#### `algebraKbarAway` (line 87)
- **Approach:** `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` per `analogies/tensoraway-instance.md` Q2.
- **Result:** RESOLVED axiom-clean. 3-line instance landed; build green.
- **Key insight:** `Mathlib` ships `Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)` but NOT
  `Algebra kbar (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; `Algebra.compHom` bridges via the algebra
  map `kbar → 𝒜 0` shipped by `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.

#### `gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap` / `gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap` (lines 659, 668)
- **Approach:** `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q3 L320-330.
- **Result:** RESOLVED axiom-clean.
- **Dead end avoided:** the analogist's alternative `MvPolynomial.aeval` route (which
  requires `Algebra` synthesis on the tensor target via more bracket-juggling) is more
  fragile; `eval₂Hom` takes a `RingHom`-shaped base, which `algebraMap kbar _` provides
  cleanly.
- **Notation trap:** `MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar` parses ambiguously
  (Lean reads `[kbar]` as a list literal); use `TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)`
  in explicit form.

#### `gmScalingP1` body skeleton (line 742)
- **Approach:** `Over.homMk` + `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over
  `(projectiveLineBarAffineCover).openCover.pullback₁ (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom)`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED with 3 named internal sorries (chart, agreement, over_coherence).
- **Body shape:** concrete `Over.homMk ((gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms _ _) _`.
- **Lessons applied from iter-170 self-pacing note:** skipped `lean_run_code` standalone
  snippet pre-verification of `Algebra.compHom`; went straight to `Edit` → `lake build` →
  `lean_diagnostic_messages`. Single iteration (after fixing the `⊗[kbar]` notation trap)
  to green build.

#### `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` (line 380)
- **Attempt 1 (cancel-surjective via named helper):** Reduced to a single focused
  surjectivity lemma. Cancel-surjective body lands axiom-clean (mod the helper sorry).
- **Result:** PARTIAL — proof body lands; surjectivity helper is the residual sorry.
- **Next step:** prove `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` via
  `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (~60-80 LOC). This sub-task is well-isolated from
  the body skeleton and can be picked up in a later iter.

## What was NOT attempted (per objectives)

- `gm_grpObj` (line 624) — 3rd-iter deferral; route via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` planned
  iter-173 (per planner). NOT touched.
- `projGm_isReduced` (line 872) — gated on `aux_left` chain; once
  `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` lands, this becomes attemptable.
  NOT touched.
- Genuine Mathlib gaps at lines 186 (`projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`), 193
  (`projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`), 842 (`gm_geomIrred`) — confirmed real gaps by
  lean-auditor iter169. NOT touched.

## What was REWRITTEN (stale docstring purge per planner note)

- **`gmScalingP1` docstring** (lines 729-741) — removed the multi-paragraph iter-169
  "PARTIAL/escalation" prose listing the two-route failure modes. New docstring is a
  stable description of the construction + reference to internal sorries.
- **`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` docstring** (lines 750-761) — removed iter-170
  escalation note; kept the mathematical sketch of the chart-1 reduction.
- **`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` docstring** (lines 374-379) — replaced TODO
  prose with a clean description of the cancel-surjective route + reference to the
  helper.

## Next-iter follow-up

### Highest leverage closures iter-172

1. **`gmScalingP1_chart kbar i` body** (line 695) — chart-`i` scheme morphism. Recipe:
   `pullbackSpecIso` ≫ `Spec.map (gmScalingP1_chart_i_ringMap)` ≫
   `Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.symm)` ≫ `Proj.awayι`. Gated on the iso's
   `aux_left` landing axiom-clean (i.e. on closing
   `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective`). ~30 LOC per chart, ~60 LOC total.

2. **`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective`** (line 372) — focused surjectivity
   via `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` specialised to `d=1, v=![X 0, X 1], dv=![1,1]`.
   ~60-80 LOC. Unblocks closure of `aux_left` axiom-clean and downstream

3. **`gmScalingP1_over_coherence`** (line 721) — once `gmScalingP1_chart` is concrete,

5. **`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`** (line 762) — chart-1 factorisation of `zeroPt` +
